member.name: Frank McNally
member.synopsis: Frank McNally is the Labour MP for Coatbridge and Bellshill , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Listed Buildings: Scotland
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, what steps she is taking to support religious organisations to make repairs to listed buildings in Scotland.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Heritage is a devolved competence, however some funders operate across the UK. To ensure continued support for listed places of worship across the UK, we are working with sector funders to maximise opportunities for all nations. The National Lottery Herit...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: Listed Buildings: Scotland
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, whether additional funding has been provided to the Scottish Government to support religious organisations following the end of the Listed Places of Worship Grant Scheme.
writtenQuestions.answerText: At the Spending Review 2025, HM Treasury agreed budgets for Departments for a three-year period for Resource DEL, and a four-year period for Capital DEL. The Department then completed a Business Planning process to allocate this funding to programmes. Thi...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: Ethnic Groups: Discrimination
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for Women and Equalities, what steps she is taking with Cabinet colleagues to help tackle discrimination against ethnic minorities.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We are taking steps to tackle discrimination by working across Government to secure a more inclusive future for everyone. We remain committed to introducing mandatory ethnicity pay gap reporting and making the right to equal pay effective for ethnic minor...

writtenQuestions.heading: Motor Neurone Disease: Research
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what steps she is taking to increase funding for motor neurone disease research.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I’m aware that my Hon. Friend recently visited the UK Dementia Research Institute at the University of Edinburgh to see their important work.We have invested £12.5 million in the Institute to support the best discovery science in MND.Since 2022, UKRI has ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Public Expenditure: Scotland
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what assessment she has made of the potential impact of the Spending Review 2025 on Scotland.
writtenQuestions.answerText: This Government has ensured the Spending Review delivers for Scotland. The Scottish Government is receiving an average of £50.9 billion per each year between 2026-27 and 2028-29, the largest real terms funding settlement since devolution. It also delivers...

writtenQuestions.heading: Surrogacy
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what his Department's timetable is for responding to the joint report of the Law Commissions of England and Wales and Scotland entitled Building families through surrogacy: a new law, published on
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government does not intend to put forward the Law Commissions’ legislative proposals at this current time, due to the limited time available for introducing new legislation. However, we will publish a Government response as time and capacity allows, a...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Energy: Prices
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what steps he is taking to reduce energy costs for domestic consumers.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to ensuring energy bills are affordable for all consumers – that’s why we are running to deliver our clean power by 2030 mission. And whilst we make this transition, we will do everything we can to support households we know ar...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Property Development: Taxation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, how much funding the Scottish Government (a) has received and (b) is forecast to receive in additional Barnett Formula consequential funding as a result of income raised via the Residential Property Developer Tax.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Residential Property Developer Tax is a UK-wide tax. UK-wide tax revenue goes into the UK Consolidated Fund. The Barnett formula then applies to changes in UK Government departmental budgets.The Scottish Government receives around 25% more funding per...

writtenQuestions.heading: Property Development: Taxation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, how much funding the Welsh Government (a) has received and (b) is forecast to receive in additional Barnett Formula consequential funding as a result of income raised via the Residential Property Developer Tax.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Residential Property Developer Tax is a UK-wide tax. UK-wide tax revenue goes into the UK Consolidated Fund. The Barnett formula then applies to changes in UK Government departmental budgets. The Welsh Government receives around 20% more funding per p...

writtenQuestions.heading: Surrogacy
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, whether he plans to respond to the report by the Law Commission of England and Wales and the Scottish Law Commission entitled Building families through surrogacy: a new law, published on 28 March 2
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Parliamentary Under-Secretary of State for Patient Safety, Women’s Health and Mental Health will be meeting with the Law Commission shortly to discuss its report and draft bill on surrogacy, and to inform consideration of the Government’s next steps.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Motor Neurone Disease: Research
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what discussions he has had with his counterparts in the devolved administrations on co-operation on research funding for motor neurone disease.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department funds research into motor neurone disease (MND) via the National Institute for Health and Care Research (NIHR). Government funding for MND research includes programmes that are working collaboratively across the United Kingdom. For example,...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Research: Motor Neurone Disease
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, when he next intends to meet with (a) Scottish Government ministers and officials, and (b) representatives from Scottish charities, universities and industry to discuss the ongoing prio
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Secretary of State has had positive early engagement with Scottish Government Ministers and looks forward to working further on their shared priorities for Scotland. The Minister for Science, Research and Innovation also met with Scottish Government M...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Research: Motor Neurone Disease
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, whether he plans to provide additional funding for motor neurone disease research in Scotland.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Funding has been distributed to 92 charities across the UK supporting 1200 doctoral students and early career researchers including over £330,000 to MND Scotland. MND research has benefited more generally from over £1.6 million of funding across the UK.Th...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology
